s.f. = A. sanā, q.v. the first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets. Its pronunciation depends on the vowel which accompanies it; e.g. ا with fatḥa (اَ) = the Devanāgarī अ = the English a, as in cedar (or = the English u in fun); ا with kasra (اِ) = the Devanāgarī इ = the English i, as in fin; ا with ẓamma (اُ) = the Devanāgarī उ = the English u, as in bull. — ا with fatḥa, followed by ا, (i.e. اَا, which is usually written آ) = the Devanāgarī आ = the English ā, as in father; ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اَو) = the Devanāgarī औ = the English au (sounded like ou in house); ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter ي, (i.e. اَي), = the Devanāgarī ऐ = the English ai, as in aisle. — ا with ẓamma, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اُو), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ऊ = the English u, as in rule (or = the oo in pool), and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ओ = the English o, as in pole. — ا with kasra, followed by ي, (i.e. اِي), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ई = the English ī, as in caprice; and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ए = the French e, as in fête. ا is used, — 1˚ (arithmetically, according to the Abjad, q.v.) for the number one. 2˚ (for अ, in Almanacks and Astrology) for Sunday, and Aries. 3˚ (for अ) as a name of the god Vishnu. 4˚ as an inseparable prefix to a word beginning with a consonant, to signify negation or privation: e.g. aśuddha, 'impure,' 'incorrect.' As in Greek, a becomes an before a vowel; e.g. ananta, 'without end,' 'endless.'

def: s.m. An idol, image (syn. but); — a mistress, lover, sweetheart; — name of a game among children (one says ṣanam āʼī, 'your sweetheart is come'; and asks 'whence?' 'in what dress?' 'what does she eat?' &c.; with whatever letter the answer to the first question begins, those to the others must begin with the same; otherwise, the person failing is condemned, by way of penalty, to imitate the voice of some animal, as a cock, or an ass, &c.)
more: n. m. صنم sculptured. 1. An idol. 2. ( Poet .) A mistress; sweetheart. 3. Children's game. ' Sanam āë .' One calls out your 'sweetheart's come.' and asks where from? in what dress? what does she eat, etc? With whatever letter the answer to the first question begins, all the other answers must begin likewise. Any one who fails in this has to imitate the cry of some animal, as a cock, donkey, etc. Sone na deṅge aur na soëṅge rāt bhar, Kheleṅge āj khel sanam kā sanam se ham . I will not sleep myself, nor let him sleep all night, I'll sanam play tonight with my fond heart's delight. sanam-khāna, sanam-kadah-i-chīn , Poet., n. m. 1. An idol temple; a pagoda. 2. See but-khānah .

(Heb. ) s. m. l. An idol. 2. A mistress, lover, sweetheart. 3. With whatever letter the answer to the first question begins, those to the others must begin with the same; otherwise, the person failing is condemned, by way of penalty, to imitate the voice of some animal, as a cock or ass, &c.

(from صنع) s. f. 1. Profession, trade, art, make, workmanship, manufacture, fabrication, work of art, machine, engine. 2. Mystery, miracle. صنعت گر ṣanʼaﻌt-gar, An artist. صنعت گري ṣanʼaﻌt-gar-ī, s. f. Art, performance of miracles.
subs: s.f. Art, artistic skill; — performance of miracles
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَنْعَت (san'at), from Arabic صَنْعَة (ṣanʕa).

def: orthodox Hinduism as opposed to new reform movements such as Arya Samaj
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit सनातन धर्म (sanātana dharma, “the eternal religion”), from सनातन (sanātana, “eternal”) + धर्म (dharma, “law”).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Eternal, continual, perpetual; firm, fixed, permanent; primeval, ancient; — epithet of Brahma; of Vishṇu; of Śiva; name of one of the four sons of Brahma
more: adj. S. सना old. Eternal; ancient. sanātan se , adv. From time immemorial. Yeh bāt sanātan se yūṅ hī chalī āī hai .
subs: adv. From of old, from time immemorial.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Having, or accompanied by, a master or protector, having a lord or husband.
more: adj. With or possessing a lord or master or husband.

def: n. m. S. सनाथ One who has a master over him.
s.f. A woman whose husband is living.
adj. Of art, artistic, &c.
s.m. The pine-tree, fir, any cone-bearing tree.
more: n. m. A cone-bearing tree; pinus pinea ( chīṛ , 4).
s. m. Fir, pine, any conebearing tree.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَنَوْبَر (sanawbar), from Arabic صَنَوْبَر (ṣanawbar).

def: s.f. pl. (of sanat), Years; — old coin current at a depreciated rate after a certain number of years (rupees of this description are commonly called sunauṭ, in opposition to sikka rupees).
more: s. plur. (of سنت) 1. Years. 2. Old coin current at a depreciated rate after a certain number of years.

def: secondary school
s.m. = سمبهالا sambhālā, q.v. s.m. The flickering of the vital flame before death, the apparent rally before death
subs: To rally before death, to show or have the last flicker of life, to flicker; to tremble in the balance (life, &c.).

def: v.n. = سمبهلنا sambhalnā, q.v. v.t. To support, sustain, hold up, uphold, prop; to take care of, to look after; to maintain; to assist, help; to set right, to arrange, settle; to manage; to superintend; to regulate (expenses, revenue, &c.); to moderate; to stop, retain; to restrain, control, curb, check, keep in check; to repress; to correct, punish
more: v. n. See saṅbhālnā , v. a. sambh'lā , n. m. A reviving crop.

def: v.t. = sambhālnā, v.t. = سمبهالنا sambhālnā, q.v. v.t. To support, sustain, hold up, uphold, prop; to take care of, to look after; to maintain; to assist, help; to set right, to arrange, settle; to manage; to superintend; to regulate (expenses, revenue, &c.); to moderate; to stop, retain; to restrain, control, curb, check, keep in check; to repress; to correct, punish
more: v. a. S. सम्भू from सं (Eng. con—) and भृ support. 1. To hold up ( thāmnā 1, 2.). 2. To assist; help. 3. To set right; arrange; settle. 4. To uphold; espouse; maintain; support. 5. To manage ( ehtimām k .). Uṭhāo merā maqnā! maiṅ ghar saṅbhālūṅ apnā! Wom. Prov. Lift up my veil! I am going to manage my house. (A satire on the new wife who exhibits haste in assuming her role of mistress, whereas conventional modesty requires her to remain completely veiled and still during the four chālas , q. v.). 6. To take care; look to or after; assure oneself of. Rupayā saṅbhāl lo! 7. To curb; check ( bāz rakhnā , 2). 8. To settle; do for; punish. Ab bole to mūṅh saṅbhāl dūṅgā! saṅbhāl'ā , n. m. The flickering of the vital flame before death. saṅbhālā lenā , v. n. To flicker; tremble in the balance. Parsoṅ saṅbhālā līyā thā, āj mar gayā. saṅbhālne-vālā . A supporter; second.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀲𑀁𑀪𑀸𑀭𑁂𑀇 (saṃbhārei, “garnishes”), 𑀲𑀁𑀪𑀸𑀮𑀺𑀅 (saṃbhālia, “supported”), from Sanskrit संभारयति (saṃbhārayati, “causes to bring together, prepares, maintains”).

def: s.m. = سمبهالو sambhālū, q.v. s.m. The three-leaved (or five-leaved) chaste-tree, Vitex trifolia and V. negundo (its leaves are used as a cure for hydrocele; and baskets are made of the twigs; — syn. meʼorī).
more: n. m. Vitex in general; the chaste tree or shrub in general. Baskets are made of the twigs, and the leaves are used as a cure for hydrocele. ujlā saṅbhālū , n. m. The white saṅbhālū; Vitex trifolia. kālā saṅbhālū , n. m. The black saṅbhālū; Vitex Negundo .

def: s.m. = sāmbhar, q.v. s.m. A kind of fossil salt extracted from a lake in the vicinity of the town of Sāmbhar (śākam-bharī) near Ajmīr (cf. sāṅbhar).
more: n. f. S. साम्बर Salt from lake Sāmbhar in Rājpūtānā. Sāṅbhar meṅ paṛā aur galā . Prov. If in the salt it fall it will be melted all. (Bad company makes bad men).

def: v.t. = سمبهارنا sambhārnā, q.v. v.t. = sambhālnā, q.v. v.t. To support, sustain, hold up, uphold, prop; to take care of, to look after; to maintain; to assist, help; to set right, to arrange, settle; to manage; to superintend; to regulate (expenses, revenue, &c.); to moderate; to stop, retain; to restrain, control, curb, check, keep in check; to repress; to correct, punish
adj. & s.m. = سچ sać, q.v.
more: adj. True, proper, correct, exact, real.
subs: prov. Fire does not harm truth; 'truth has nothing to fear' (prob. in allusion to ordeal by fire).

def: adj. = sać, and saććā, qq.v. adj. & s.m. = sāṅć, and sać, qq.v. adj. & s.m. = سچ sać, q.v.
more: n. m. 1. A mould; matrix. 2. The womb. sāṅche meṅ ḍhāl' nā , v. n. To mould; form; shape. sāṅche kī sachchī , Slang. n. f. A woman too prone to conceive.
adj. True, proper, correct, exact, real.
s. m. A matrice, a cone ( Harris ).
subs: prov. Fire does not harm truth; 'truth has nothing to fear' (prob. in allusion to ordeal by fire).

def: s.m. A mould, matrice; figure, form, outline; a cone
subs: (-ko), To cast in a mould, to mould, form, shape.

def: part. (of sanćnā) = sanćit, q.v.
subs: sanćā karnā = sanćit karnā. part. Heaped up
accumulated, collected, gathered, saved, hoarded; — s.m. A collection; a code
v.t. To heap up, to gather up, to collect, accumulate, hoard.

ety: S. सञ्चलः, rt. चल् with सम्
def: s.m. (dialec.) Noise, sound of footsteps.

ety: S. सञ्चयनं, or v.n. fr. saṅćnā
def: s.m. Collecting, gathering, amassing, hoarding

s.m. Passing through, passage, progress, transition, course, going, motion; a passage, way, road, gate, doorway, entrance, opening, access; the passage or entrance of the sun (or a planet) into a new sign; — difficult progress; difficulty, distress; — setting in motion; impelling, inciting; leading, guiding; conveying, transmission, communication (of disease), contagion; — a gem supposed to be in the head of a serpent.
more: s. m. 1. Entrance, penetration. 2. Beginning, commencement. 3. The entering of the planets into a new sign.

def: s.f. A female messenger, a go-between, procuress, bawd; — a pair, brace; — odour, smell.
more: s. f. A bawd, a procuress.

ety: saṅćar˚ = S. सञ्चर(ति), rt. चर् with सम्
def: v.n. To go, proceed, be in motion.
s.m. Crystallized salt; Epsom salts; Glauber's salt.
s.m. Collection, gathering, heaping up, accumulation, heap, hoard, saving, store, purse, stock, capital; multitude, number, quantity
more: s. m. Purse, capital, stock, store, saving, heaping up, hoard.
subs: (-kā), To collect, to amass, to keep in store, store up, hoard, save, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. Collecting, gathering, accumulating, hoarding; — one who accumulates or hoards, amasser, hoarder.
adj. Composed, collected; attentive, mindful, conscious (of), cautious, alive (to).
more: adj. 1. Composed; collected. 2. Attentive; intent; cautious; alive to.

def: n. m. A quantity; number (اتَالا 1.).

adj. = saṅkshep, q.v. s.m. Throwing together; condensing; compression; conciseness, brevity; an abridgement, summary, abbreviation, abstract, compendium, epitome, précis, syllabus; — adj. Condensed, abridged; brief, concise
subs: (-kā, or -ko), To condense, to abridge, to epitomize, make a summary (of), &c.
saṅkshep yah or saṅkshep yah hai
The short (of it) is this; in short, in brief, in fine.

def: s.m. = saṅkshepan, q.v. s.m. Throwing together; condensing; compression; conciseness, brevity; an abridgement, summary, abbreviation, abstract, compendium, epitome, précis, syllabus; — adj. Condensed, abridged; brief, concise

saṅkshep yah or saṅkshep yah hai
The short (of it) is this; in short, in brief, in fine.

def: s.m. = sākshī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Observing, witnessing; attesting, testifying, bearing testimony, giving evidence; — observer, eye-witness, witness (in law)
subs: or sākshī karnā (-ko), To give (one) as a witness, to make (one) a witness, to call (one) to witness.

def: part. Heaped up, accumulated, collected, gathered, saved, hoarded; — s.m. A collection; a code
more: n. m. सम् together, and चित gathered. A code. sanchit , adj. Hoarded; collected. san'chah k., sanchnā , v. a. To collect; bring together.
subs: v.t. To heap up, to gather up, to collect, accumulate, hoard.

def: s.f. The plant Salvinia cucullata.

def: v.t. To collect, gather, accumulate, heap up, save, amass, hoard.
(Braj) = sāṅćā, q.v. adj. = sać, and saććā, qq.v. adj. & s.m. = sāṅć, and sać, qq.v. adj. & s.m. = سچ sać, q.v.
subs: prov. Fire does not harm truth; 'truth has nothing to fear' (prob. in allusion to ordeal by fire).

def: s.m. = śaṇḍ, and sāṅḍ, qq.v.; — 2˚ adj. = saṇḍā. s.m. A bull (esp. one set at liberty as an act of piety, and allowed to wander about at will); — a stallion; — an independent, extravagant fellow (syn. ćhal-ćikaniyā)
s.m. A bull (esp. one set at liberty as an act of piety, and allowed to wander about at will); — a stallion; — an independent, extravagant fellow (syn. ćhal-ćikaniyā)
more: s. m. 1. A bull. 2. A stallion. 3. ( met. ) An independent, extravagant fellow.

def: n. m. S. शण्ड. 1. A bull set at liberty as an act of piety, and privileged to wander about at will. 2. A stallion. Javān rāṅḍ, būṛhe sāṅ! Prov. The young as widows cold, as lusty bulls the old! 3. An independent, or extravagant fellow. 4. ( Contemp .) A lecherous fellow. rānḍ kā sāṅḍ, lit . A widow's bull. ( Contemp .) A widow's son, usually a spoilt child.
s.m. A bull at liberty; a eunuch; an impotent man; a hermaphrodite; a clown; a collection of lotus flowers; a multitude; a wood, thicket.
s.m. A rope by which a cow's legs are tied at the time of milking it.
more: n. m. An animal like a lizard. The oil is believed to be a cure for gout and impotence. Tel hai sāṅḍe kā! Kanjars' cry.

def: s.m. A species of sand-lizard (com. called ūsar-sāṅḍā; — the oil extracted from it is supposed to be a cure for rheumatism, gout, and impotence).
more: s. m. Name of an animal resembling a lizard (the oil of which is said to have restorative powers).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Big, fat, burly, stout, strong, sturdy; — s.m. A big or fat ox; — hardened excrement, scybala
more: 1. adj. Strong, fat, stout. 2. s. m. Hardened excrement, Scybala.
subs: v.n. To become fat and big, to grow stout and sturdy, &c. ( = saṇḍiyānā)
or saṇḍ-musaṇḍ, adj. Fat, plump, well-developed, stout, &c. ( = saṇḍā).

def: s.m. An anvil ( = sandān, q.v.); a broad stone; fetid excrement ( = sanḍā, q.v.).
more: adj. S. शण्ड Stout; muscular; sinewy; strong; lusty. Mulkoṅ meṅ sarnām ki jinke adhik birājeṅ jhande , Play. Jitne chele Gur-nānak ke sadā bane raheṅ sanḍe! Full-famed in every country round the flag which waves so bright, Be stout and strong ye followers of Gur Nānak behight! sanḍā-musṭanḍā, sanḍ-musand , adj. Plump; well-developed ( sanḍā ).

def: s.m. = صندل ṣandal, q.v.
s.m. Sandal-wood, sanders; — a perfumed embrocation obtained by rubbing a piece of sandal-wood with water on a stone (called ṣandalāsā)
more: n. m. Sandal wood ( chandan ). sandal ke chhāpe mūṅh par lagnā , v. n. To come off with flying colors. sandal kī sī takhtī , adj. As smooth and soft as sandal wood. sandal ghisnā , Mah. Wom., v. a. To use the dildo. Ā, dugānā! chhātiyoṅ se chhātīyaṅ mal mal ghiseṅ! Chal, badan ko ham badan par ragṛeṅ, aur sandal ghiseṅ! Wom. Rangīn.
(from Sans. चंदन ) s. m. Sandal wood, sanders.

s. m. Sandal wood. (The word often means a perfumed embrocation, obtained by rubbing a piece of sandal wood with water on a stone called ṣandlāsā: Qanoon-e-islam, glossary, p. xcviii). صندل کا کهور ṣandal kā khor, (dakh.) The heart of the sandal wood ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: vulg. ṣandlāsā, s.m. A flat circular stone on which sandal-wood is ground
s.f. = ṣandal-āsā, q.v.; — (and) a slab for grinding spices or drugs on
(kisī-ke), 'To have marks of ground sandal-wood made on the face'; to be distinguished; to come off with honour or with flying colours.

def: s.m. A dealer in sandal-wood a seller of perfumes and drugs.
s.m. A flat stone on which sandal-wood is ground ( = ṣandlāsā).
more: s. m. A stone on which sandal wood is ground.

def: adj. & s.f. = صندلي ṣandalī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Made of sandal-wood; of the colour of sandal-wood, light-yellow; anything coloured with sandal-wood; — a eunuch whose parts of generation have been entirely extracted.
more: adj. 1. Of the color of sandal wood; light yellow. 2. Made of sandal wood. sandl'ī , n. f. A movable scaffold. sandlī , n. m. A eunuch whose parts of generation have been entirely extracted.
1. adj. Of the colour of sandal wood; brown, sorrel. 2. Made of sandal wood. 3. s. f. A kind of chair. 4. s. m. A sort of eunuch.

r: ṣandalī, ṣandlī, ṣandal, sandal, lit., sandalī
def: (fr. ṣandal, for orig. sandal, 'a sandal, a boot'), s.f. lit. 'The place for the sandals or boots'; a bench, a seat; a movable scaffold; (it is said to have been the custom in old times to place the boots or shoes of princes and nobles on a bench in an ante-chamber; whence the term sandalī).
s.m. Rope, cord (esp. for tying cattle); — an elephant's temples, or the part whence the ichorous fluid issues.

ety: prob. = S. सन्धान; but said to be A., and to be derived fr. سند; see sanad
def: s.f. An anvil; the iron on which the knocker of a door strikes.
s.f. A cow-house.
s.m. Pincers, &c. ( = saṅṛsī, q.v.). s.f. A pair of pincers or nippers, small tongs, forceps (see saṅṛsī).
magnificent, excellent

def: s.m. Sandarach, a resin obtained from Thuja articulata or Callitris quadrivalvis (a tree growing in Barbary); — the proto-sulphuret of arsenic, red arsenic, realgar.
more: n. m. Sandarak or sandaracha. A resin supposed to be produced by the juníperus communis , but now proved to exude from a species of Thuia ( Thuia articulata ). (?).
s. m. 1. Sandarak, or Sandaracha; a resin supposed to be produced by the Juniperus communis, but now proved to exude from a species of Thuia (Thuia articulata: Vahl ).

def: s.m. Looking steadfastly, gazing, viewing, beholding, seeing, sight, vision; seeing, one another, meeting; appearance, presence; regard, consideration, reflection; — causing to see, showing, presenting.

s.m. Looking steadfastly, gazing, viewing, beholding, seeing, sight, vision; seeing, one another, meeting; appearance, presence; regard, consideration, reflection; — causing to see, showing, presenting.
s.m. A cesspool, latrine, necessary, water-closet (commonly on the roof of a house, and communicating with a pool or drain under the house); a sink, sewer.
more: s. m. A necessary, a watercloset (opening into the streets), a sink.

def: n. m. S. सन्दंश A necessary; a water-closet (opening into the streets); sink; sewer; latrine.
s.m. A pair of tongs or nippers, large pincers.
n. f. S. सन्दंशिनी 1. Pincers used to take down the heated cooking vessel from the fire place. 2. ( Mech .) Callipers.
s.f. A pair of pincers or nippers, small tongs, forceps (see saṅṛsī).

s.m. corr. of sannyāsī, q.v. s.m. One who abandons or resigns; one who has abandoned all worldly possessions and affections, an ascetic, a devotee; a Brāhmaṇ of the fourth order or āśram, a religious mendicant.
more: s. m. A cast of Hindū faḳīrs.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = sandā and sanḍsī, qq.v. s.m. A rope by which a cow's legs are tied at the time of milking it.
s. m. Suspicion, doubt, apprehension, hesitation, anxiety.
s.m. Doubt,uncertainty, suspense, hesitation, apprehension, mistrust, suspicion, anxiety; risk, danger; grief, sorrow
more: n. m. S. सन्देह = सम् + दिह to be uncertain. 1. Doubt (انديشه 1, 2.). 1. Dukh sandehā sab merā bhājā, jib maiṅ yoh udmī dekhī, Supne māṅ sauk marī dekhī . Rus. Wom. My pains all from me fled, with joy unlimited My co-wife in my dream I saw before me dead! 2. He bahinī, kāhe sandeh katti hau? tumhār bālam jaldī bahuriëṅ . E. Rus. Wom. Sister! why do you fret so? Your husband will soon come back again. 2. Grief; sorrow. sandeh k. , v. a. To grieve; fret. nis-sandeh , adv. Undoubtedly; certainly.
subs: (-meṅ, or -kā?), To doubt; to be apprehensive (about); &c.

def: adj. & s.m. Doubtful, dubious; doubting; suspicious, mistrustful, apprehensive; scrupulous; — a doubter; a mistrustful person, &c.
adj. Doubtful; suspicious.
s.m. (dialec.) = sandeh; — adj. = sandehī.
s.m. See سنديس sandes.
s.m. Information, news, tidings, intelligence, report; message, commission, command, direction; embassy; a present.
s.m. Information, news, tidings, intelligence, report; message, commission, command, direction; embassy; a present.
more: n. m. S. सन्देश. 1. A message; tidings (خبر 1.). 1. Bāloṅ hāth chhinālā, aur kāgoṅ hāth saṅdes . Prov. Boys messages of wantons take, crows news bring or enquiries make. 2. Khāli hāth kyā jāūṅ, ek saṅdesā letā jāūṅ! Prov. Not empty handed should I go, a message I will take. 3. Uṛ uṛ rī chiṛyā sulachhnī, ek saṅdesā le jāë! Kahiyo mere bāp se beṭī lehū bulāë! Fly, fly, good bird, a message now just bear from me! Say to my father, 'for your daughter send prythee!' 2. Embassy ( paigām ). 3. Present.

def: s.m. = sandesī, q.v. s.m. (f. -inī), One who brings news, a messenger, an envoy, an ambassador.
s.m. (f. -inī), One who brings news, a messenger, an envoy, an ambassador.
s.m. (dialec.) = sandes, and sandesī, qq.v. s.m. Information, news, tidings, intelligence, report; message, commission, command, direction; embassy; a present.
s.m. = sandesu.
s.m. = sandhaw, q.v. s.m. A kind of salt, rock-salt.
more: s. m. The name of a kind of salt, rock-salt.

def: s.m. The act of placing or holding together, joining, junction, union, tying, binding, fixing (an arrow to a bow, &c.), arranging; association, league, combination, alliance; companionship, company; peace, repairing, restoration; composing, compounding, mixing, preparation; drawing together, contraction (as of the skin by astringents. &c.); advertence, attention; sustaining, supporting; reception, receiving; — an aim; guess, discovery; search, inquiry, prying into secrets, spying; a secret; information about a lost thing or person; — distillation; spirituous liquor; pickles; a relish, something eaten to excite thirst; sour rice-gruel
more: s. m. Spying, prying into secrets. سندهان پانا sandhān pānā, v. a. To trace, to discover.
subs: (-kā), To obtain a clue (to), to track, trace, discover
v.t. To arrange; to prepare; to fix (an arrow to a bow), to take aim, &c.

def: s.m. = H سندهاني सन्धानी sandhānī[S.सन्धानिका], s.f. Pickle, acid preparation of bel and other fruits.
s.f. = H سندهانا सन्धाना sandhānā [S.सन्धान+कः], s.m. Pickle, acid preparation of bel and other fruits.
s.f. Distilling, distillation; — a foundry, a place where metals are wrought or stored.
s.m. A kind of salt, rock-salt.

s.f. Conjunction, junction, combination, union, contact; composition, construction, structure; compact, agreement, association, alliance, league, company; reconciliation, peace, making a treaty of peace, negotiating an alliance, pacification; a treaty; (in Gram.) euphonic combination or coalescence of the final letter of a word with the initial letter of a following word; a connecting link; a joint, articulation (of the body, &c.); a fold; an interval, pause, rest, stop; — critical juncture, opportune moment; — dividing, breaking; division, separation; breach, chasm, mine, hole, opening or cavity made in a wall or underneath a building (by a housebreaker, &c.); vagina, vulva; a period at the expiration of each yuga or age (equivalent to one-sixth of its duration, and intervening before the commencement of the next)
more: n. f. 1. Conjunction. 2. ( Gram .) Euphonic junction of final and initial letters; euphony.
subs: s.m. One who brings about union, or pacification, &c.; reconciler, peace-maker.

def: part. Joined or fastened together, bound, tied, united, strung; fixed, fitted, prepared; reconciled, allied.

v.t. (dialec.) = sādhnā, q.v. v.t. To accomplish, effect, perform; to observe, keep; to practise, to use; to prepare, make, shape, fashion, mould; to familiarize gradually (to any habit), to habituate, accustom; to teach; train, discipline; to learn, to acquire; to show, to prove, demonstrate, substantiate; to settle, fix, determine; to regulate, order, arrange; to correct, rectify, improve, ameliorate; to modulate; to steady, balance, poise; to point (a gun); to conciliate, pacify; to propitiate, adore, worship; to hold or stop (the breath)
subs: v.t. (intens.) To prove, test, examine the correctness (of).

def: s.f. def: adj. Stony, of stone, made of stone; hard; firm, solid; strong; thick, stout, close-woven; heavy, weighty; grave, serious; severe (e.g. sazā-ě-saṅgīn, 'severe punishment'), excessive; — s.f. A bayonet (in this sense supposed to be derived from Turkish süngyü)
more: 1. adj. Stony, of stone. 2. Heavy, weighty. 3. Solid, thick. 4. s. f. A bayonet. سنگين دل sangīn-dil, adj. Hardhearted. سنگين دلي sangīn-dil-ī, s. f. Hardheartedness.

def: adj. See sakht , 1, 4, 5, 6, and bhārī (1, 2, 4, 9.) 1. Of stone; strong. 2. Grave; aggravated; serious; heinous. 3. Thick; close-woven. Sangīn ḳapṛā nikālo! sangīn' , n. f. A Turkish süṅgyü ; a bayonet. saṅgīn jamٴa-bandī , n. A heavy assessment.

ety: saṅgīn, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Heaviness; a load; firmness, solidity; hardness, severity; excessiveness, excess.
more: n. f. 1. Hardness; aggravation, etc. 2. Solidity; firmness. sangīnī-i-jamٴa , G. G. Over-assessment.

def: s. m. Stony place or region.
s.m. = sangīt, q.v.
s.m. A song in chorus, chorus; a public entertainment consisting of song, music, and dancing, a concert; the art of singing with music and dancing
more: s. m. 1. The exhibition of song, dancing and musick as a publick entertainment. 2. The art or science of musick and dancing. سنگيت درپن sangīt-darpan, Name of a book on musick. سنگيت ناچ sangīt-nāch, A kind of dance.
subs: s.m. lit. 'Mirror of harmony,' name of a work on music

def: s.m. (dialec.) = saṅg, q.v. s.f. Spear, javelin, dart (all of iron), a goad; — a borer for wells.
s.m. See s.v. saṅg, 'stone.' s.f. Spear, javelin, dart (all of iron), a goad; — a borer for wells.
more: n. A smallish bird of a dark color, called in Arabic qatā .
s. A kind of bird, a smallish bird of a dark colour, called in Arabic قطاة ḳat̤āt (?).

def: s.f. (local) See s.v. saṅg, 'stone.' s.f. Spear, javelin, dart (all of iron), a goad; — a borer for wells.
s.m. (dialec.) = sāṅkho, q.v. s.m. A bridge, causeway, suspension-bridge (syn. pul).
s.m. Hiding, concealing, complete concealment.

ety: saṅgor˚ = S. सङ्गूढ, p.p.p. of rt. सम्+गुह्
def: v.t. To collect, store, lay up, amass, hoard.
s.m. See s.v. saṅg, 'stone.' s.f. Spear, javelin, dart (all of iron), a goad; — a borer for wells.
s. m. A turtle, tortoise.

s.m. Taking, seizing; grasping; clenching (the fist); reception, admission, assent; guarding, protecting, protection, favouring, supporting, encouraging; propitiation; entertainment; bringing together, conjunction, collection, compilation, code, catalogue, abridgement, epitome, summary; sum, amount, totality; restraining, controlling, governing; effort, exertion; mentioning, mention; greatness; elevation, loftiness
more: n. m. A collection; compilation; code. sangrah k., saṅgvānā , v. a To scrape together; garner up; convene ( ikhaṭṭā k . 1).
subs: and saṅgrah-karne-hārā, s.m. Seizer; collector, compiler, &c.
